title: create_category bumps Category state_seq on INSERT OR IGNORE no-op
description: daemon/src/store.rs:1047-1055: create_category executes INSERT OR IGNORE then unconditionally calls self.bump_state_seq("Category"). If the category id already exists the INSERT is a no-op, but the state token is still incremented. Clients polling Category/changes will see a spurious state change with an empty diff, triggering unnecessary re-fetches. Root cause: same pattern as nie-kef6.24 (Space), nie-kef6.25 (SpaceMember), nie-14sh.44 (Chat). Fix: capture rows_affected() from the execute() result and skip bump_state_seq when affected == 0.
